target.title: fix(agent): use static catalog for skip-pi model resolution
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes the skip-PI model-resolution path so known provider-owned models can use the bundled static catalog before PI models.json is generated.
- This prevents known Google Vertex model refs such as google-vertex/gemini-2.5-pro from surfacing a misleading missing models.providers registration error when the static catalog already knows the model.
- Adds regression coverage for the Google Vertex run path.

## Linked context
Closes #88816

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: google-vertex/gemini-2.5-pro could fail before reply with an Unknown model / missing provider registration error while skipPiDiscovery was active.
- Real environment tested: Linux contributor checkout of OpenClaw, focused runner test harness.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: corepack pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts -- --reporter=dot
- Evidence after fix: the focused Vitest shard passed with 96 tests, including the new regression asserting the google-vertex run path passes allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback with skipPiDiscovery.
- Observed result after fix: skip-PI resolution is permitted to consult the bundled static catalog for known model refs.
- What was not tested: a live Google Vertex API-key request against the external service.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: I do not have a safe Google Vertex API key/project in this contributor environment, so the live provider call is left for maintainer or reporter confirmation.
- Before evidence: issue #88816 includes the reported failing error for google-vertex/gemini-2.5-pro and google-vertex/gemini-3.5-flash.

## Tests and validation
- corepack pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts -- --reporter=dot
- corepack pnpm oxfmt src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
- git diff --check

## Risk checklist
- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, known static-catalog models can resolve earlier in the skip-PI path instead of waiting for PI discovery or erroring.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No secrets or network behavior changes; this only allows local bundled model metadata fallback during model resolution.
- Highest-risk area: resolving a static-catalog model slightly earlier than before. Mitigation: the fallback only applies to known bundled catalog entries, and existing full PI discovery remains available for runtime-generated models.

## Current review state
- Next action: maintainer review and, ideally, reporter/maintainer confirmation with a real Google Vertex API-key profile.
- Waiting on: CI and any maintainer-requested real-provider proof.
- Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.
target.author: charles-openclaw
